dialogues.dialogue: It's a sin to reveal her crime in front of girls like you. I'd still like to know. She's shameless. She had an illicit relation with her husband. So what? - What do you mean?
'Hurry up. Hurry up.' 'Leave the magazine here. I'll read it. Give it to me.' 'What a shame! You too?' 'I should have died before seeing all this.' Prabha! Where are you going?
dialogues.cause_raw: Reading a magazine implied inappropriate content)

dialogues.dialogue: Great, I get beaten up and you take the credit. This is the certificate of my honesty, Mrs. Prabha Bharat Prasad. It's a proud feeling for me that the most dishonest person of this city believed that I am an honest man.
dialogues.cause_raw: being perceived as honest by a dishonest person
